name: Tangleweaver
flavor: The battlefield-control discipline of the wire: a Puppeteer who stops stringing up creatures one at a time and starts filling whole patches of ground with wire, so that walking into the show at all becomes the mistake.

features.name: Snare Weave
features.description: As an action, you weave loose wire across the ground in a 15-foot square within 60 feet of you, filling it with a low, near-invisible lattice that lasts for 1 minute or until you dismiss it (no action required). The area is difficult terrain to any creature without a String attached to you. Any creature other than you that enters the area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a Charisma saving throw against your control DC or become restrained by the wire. A creature restrained this way can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the restrained condition on itself on a success, or use its action to make a Strength or Dexterity check (its choice) against your control DC to tear free instead. You can use Snare Weave a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum once), regaining all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Binding Knots
features.description: Your bindings compound on themselves. When you use Tangle, you can target one additional creature you have a String attached to within range, provided the two targets are within 15 feet of each other and you don't mix a willing target with an unwilling one in the same use. You can use this double-target option a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum once), regaining all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
